The Ghost Writer (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $15,541,549Details
International Box Office $49,990,942
Worldwide Box Office $65,532,491
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,313,955
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,337,352
Total $6,651,307
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When a successful ghostwriter, the Ghost, agrees to finish the memoirs of Adam Long, England’s former prime minister, his publisher assures him it’s the chance of a lifetime. Instead, he begins to uncover evidence that suggests his late predecessor knew a dark secret about Lang and may have been murdered to prevent it from coming to light.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$183,009 (1.2% of total gross)
Legs:7.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:23.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$45,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/819 max. theaters, 6.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $23,558,545

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 19th, 2010 (Limited) by Summit Entertainment
Video Release: August 3rd, 2010 by Summit Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for language, brief nudity/sexuality, some violence and a drug reference
(Rating bulletin 2105, 1/27/2010)
Running Time: 129 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Unnamed Character
Plot point: Assassination, C.I.A., Political, Political Assassination, Writing and Writers
Social setting: Film Noir
Source material: Inspired by a True Story
Subgenre: Political Thriller
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Summit Entertainment, R.P. Films, France 2 Cinema, Elfte Babelsberg Film GmbH, Runteam II Limited
Production Countries: United Kingdom, France, Germany
Languages: English

Synopsis

When a successful ghostwriter, the Ghost, agrees to finish the memoirs of Adam Long, England’s former prime minister, his publisher assures him it’s the chance of a lifetime. Instead, he begins to uncover evidence that suggests his late predecessor knew a dark secret about Lang and may have been murdered to prevent it from coming to light.
